title: Vault Overlay Registry (additive overlay for doc-dependencies skeleton)
description: Validates ${CLAUDE_HOME}/hooks/config/vault-overlay.json — the per-vault additive overlay that pairs with the foundation-shipped doc-dependencies.json skeleton. Same structural shape as doc-dependencies.json; semantics are additive-only at runtime: consumers (pre-write-guard.sh, librarian rename-history-sync/wikilink-repair/entity-parity capabilities, session-close Step 2c) merge overlay.entries[] and overlay.entities{} on top of skeleton entries/entities. Overlay rows by .id replace skeleton rows of the same id (allows local override of a foundation entry); overlay ids not present in skeleton are appended. Overlay entities keys merge into skeleton entities map (overlay wins on conflict, mirroring entries semantics). Adopter-facing walkthrough (skeleton vs overlay, merge precedence, hand-editing, known constraints): docs/doc-dependencies-conventions.md. Authored by Plan 81 SP01 T-28a (2026-05-08) ratifying Alternative (b) vault-overlay split per Plan 71 _audit-2026-05-05/04-librarian-architect-personalization.md G4 → SP12 skeleton-only resolution. Pairs with doc-dependencies.json (https://stem.peter.dev/schemas/doc-dependencies/v1.json — schema authoring deferred; structural shape mirrored here as authoritative reference).

version.description: Overlay registry version. Locked at 2 to mirror doc-dependencies.json version field; consumers reject mismatched versions to surface schema drift early.

description.description: Human-readable description of overlay purpose. Recommended: declare the vault this overlay applies to + author + datestamp.

generated_by.description: Provenance string. May be an onboarding-flow tag, a session reference, or a manual-author tag. Foundation install ships this empty.

schema_notes.description: Free-form notes about overlay-local conventions. Not consumed by runtime; documentation surface for the overlay author.

entries.description: Additive cascade-rule entries. Merged into doc-dependencies.json skeleton entries[] at runtime. Empty array (default) ships with install.

id.description: Stable identifier. Overlay row with id matching a skeleton id replaces the skeleton row (consumer-side override semantics).

kind.description: Entry kind. Common values: directory-write-constraint, directory-membership-cascade, hub-spoke-cascade, cross-file-type-union. Unconstrained for forward-compatible additions; consumers tolerate unknown kinds with advisory warnings.

primary_dir.description: Primary directory the entry governs (for directory-* kinds). Mutually exclusive with primary.

primary.description: Primary file path the entry governs (for hub-* / cross-* kinds). Mutually exclusive with primary_dir.

primary_scope.description: Scope qualifier for directory-membership-cascade (e.g., top-level-children, recursive).

mirrors.description: Mirror entries that must stay synchronized with primary. Heterogeneous field-set per kind.

rule.description: Rule label consumed by pre-write-guard advisory emission (e.g., if-primary-changes-mirrors-must-be-reviewed, hub-stub-must-have-matching-spoke, deny-list).

severity.description: Advisory severity. warn = inform on stderr, allow write; deny = block write.

denied_types.description: deny-list-kind entries: vault-schema type values denied in the primary_dir scope.

rationale.description: Human-readable rationale for the entry. Surfaced in advisory messages for traceability.

notes.description: Free-form notes for entry maintainers. Not consumed by runtime.

path_inferred_exceptions.description: vault-schema type values whose path-inference exempts them from the entry's union check (cross-file-type-union kind).

rename_history.description: Append-only rename audit trail. Populated by librarian rename-detect at session-close Step 2b. Empty array means no known renames since instrumentation landed.

entities.description: Additive entity-parity registry. Merged into doc-dependencies.json skeleton entities{} at runtime. Overlay keys override skeleton keys of the same name. Empty object (default) ships with install. Underscore-prefixed string keys (_comment, _note, _schema_version, etc.) are permitted as inline documentation and ignored by consumers; the live doc-dependencies.json convention uses _comment for entity-registry header docs.

^_.description: Underscore-prefixed metadata key (e.g., _comment, _schema_version). Ignored by consumers.

canonical.description: Path template with {placeholder} tokens. Per-vault placeholders (e.g., {project_namespace}) require consumer-side resolution against user-manifest.json or environment.

canonical_field.description: Field name on canonical-source frontmatter that consumers compare against mirror values.

instance_enumerator.description: Enumerator strategy for entity instances. Format: <strategy>:<arg> (e.g., glob:~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md).

exclude_basenames.description: Basenames to exclude from instance enumeration (e.g., MEMORY.md for the memory-file entity).

mirrors.description: Mirror surfaces consumers verify against canonical.
